findings.problem: At several hinge moments the close-third narration explains Beni's submission in polished abstract language instead of letting his materially grounded service-worker sensorium and bodily shame carry it. That drifts away from the style bible's Beni voice and makes him sound more analytically fluent in himself than he should here.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive clusters anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:229, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:357. Keep the asymmetry and repeated-wound logic, but move at least two of those beats into concrete car/job/body detail: what he notices, what he does not say, what the seat, tie, house, or mirror make him feel, rather than a full thesis sentence about what the scene means.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:207 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:303 let a reader infer why Beni stays and keeps going from concrete physical or service-detail evidence, and the closing span around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:357 contains no more than one direct abstract summary sentence about what the night 'was'. Fail if all three areas still spell out the power dynamic in interpretive nouns instead of scene-specific detail.

findings.problem: The chapter leans repeatedly on the same negation-then-correction sentence shape. Used once, it sharpens emphasis; used this many times in one chapter, it becomes a visible prose tic that flattens rhythm and advertises authorial control.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:233, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:357. Preserve the meaning, but let at least three of these beats land through different syntactic strategies: direct statement, image, action, or a concrete consequence instead of the same negation-reversal template.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one of the cited passages still uses a clear 'not X / but Y' reversal as its main sentence engine when read in context. Fail if three or more of those four beats still hinge on the same negation-correction construction.

summary: The chapter clears the hard requirements on structure and dialogue: Jonas humiliates Nina in public social terms, the garage sex is explicit and asymmetrical rather than sanitized, narrative tense stays in past, and the speakers keep distinct, contraction-appropriate registers. Craft and prose still fail because the chapter repeatedly over-interprets its own power dynamic in abstract thematic language and leans too often on the same negation-reversal sentence template, which dulls a scene that is otherwise materially vivid and consequential.
